description: CWE vulnerability patterns for binary analysis

patterns.name: Buffer Copy without Checking Size (strcpy)
patterns.description: Use of strcpy without bounds checking may lead to buffer overflow

patterns.name: Buffer Copy without Checking Size (strcat)
patterns.description: Use of strcat without bounds checking may lead to buffer overflow

patterns.name: Buffer Copy without Checking Size (gets)
patterns.description: Use of gets() is always vulnerable to buffer overflow

patterns.name: Buffer Copy without Checking Size (sprintf)
patterns.description: Use of sprintf without bounds checking may lead to buffer overflow

patterns.name: Unbounded memcpy
patterns.description: memcpy with sizeof operand — verify bounds are checked

patterns.name: Format String Vulnerability (printf)
patterns.description: printf-family called with user-controlled format string

patterns.name: Format String Vulnerability (syslog)
patterns.description: syslog called with user-controlled format string

patterns.name: Format String Vulnerability (wprintf)
patterns.description: wprintf-family with potentially user-controlled format string

patterns.name: Integer Overflow before Allocation
patterns.description: Arithmetic expression in allocation size may overflow

patterns.name: Integer Overflow (Addition before Allocation)
patterns.description: Addition in allocation size may wrap around

patterns.name: Use After Free Hint
patterns.description: Potential use-after-free: pointer used after free()

patterns.name: Double Free Hint
patterns.description: Same pointer may be freed twice

patterns.name: OS Command Injection (system)
patterns.description: system() call may allow command injection

patterns.name: OS Command Injection (WinExec)
patterns.description: WinExec may allow command injection

patterns.name: OS Command Injection (popen)
patterns.description: popen may allow command injection if argument is user-controlled

patterns.name: OS Command Injection (ShellExecute)
patterns.description: ShellExecute with user-controlled parameters may allow command injection

patterns.name: DLL Hijacking (Relative LoadLibrary)
patterns.description: LoadLibrary with relative path is vulnerable to DLL hijacking

patterns.name: DLL Hijacking (Variable LoadLibrary)
patterns.description: LoadLibrary with variable argument may be vulnerable to DLL hijacking

patterns.name: Use of Potentially Dangerous Function
patterns.description: Use of function known to have dangerous behaviors

patterns.name: Inherently Dangerous Function
patterns.description: Function is inherently dangerous with no safe usage
